story.title: Refactor v0.1 globals into Store/View/Yaml/Validate/Persist/Platform namespaces with normalized-canonical coordinates
story.as_a: DevCrew engineer building every subsequent v0.2 feature
story.i_want: a single workspace state object mutated only through Store actions, with normalized coordinates as the source of truth and all I/O behind a Platform adapter
story.so_that: point editing, YAML import, persistence, and multi-camera can each be built without touching each other's code, and the Electron v0.3 port only replaces Platform

acceptance_criteria: Given the refactored app, when a user performs every v0.1 workflow (upload image, draw zone/motion mask/object mask, close, name, undo, clear, delete item, delete all, copy YAML, download YAML, add/delete camera, camera YAML), then behavior and YAML output are byte-identical to v0.1 except documented fixes

Given any polygon, when the canvas is re-rendered at a different display scale or a new image of different resolution is loaded, then pixel positions are derived from normalized x,y (3-decimal) and the emitted YAML coordinates do not change

Given a UI event handler in the script, when it needs to change state, then it calls a Store action (e.g., Store.addPoint, Store.closePolygon, Store.deletePolygon, Store.addCamera) and never mutates workspace fields or calls draw()/generateYaml() directly — rendering and YAML regeneration occur only via Store.subscribe

Given v0.1's item shape ({mode:'object_mask',...}), when migrateV1(items, cameras) runs, then it returns a schemaVersion:2 workspace with type:'object_filter_mask' naming, generated polygon ids, and polygons assigned to the 'default' pseudo-camera
Given the refactored file, when opened from file:// with network disabled, then no console errors occur and no network requests are made
constraints: Single HTML file preserved; no build step; no framework; no canvas library
Namespaces are plain JS objects in labeled script sections, not ES modules
Normalized coordinates (0–1, rounded to 3 decimals at YAML-emit time, full precision in state) are canonical; px/py never stored on points
Platform = { readImageFile(file), copyText(text), downloadFile(name, text) } is the only place touching FileReader, clipboard, or Blob/anchor downloads
Replace deprecated document.execCommand('copy') with navigator.clipboard.writeText inside Platform.copyText with execCommand fallback for file:// contexts
switchTab must take the clicked element explicitly, not read global event
edge_cases: YAML copy invoked before any polygon exists — empty string, no error
Image re-uploaded with different resolution while polygons exist — polygons re-derive pixel positions from normalized coords and remain visually correct relative to frame
navigator.clipboard unavailable (insecure context/file://) — fallback path used, no thrown exception
Store.subscribe callbacks must not be able to re-enter actions infinitely — actions notify once, synchronously, after mutation completes
risks: Medium: behavior regressions during refactor — mitigated by AC1 manual parity checklist run by QA
Low: full-precision normalized storage changes 3rd-decimal rounding vs v0.1 in rare cases — acceptable, document in QA notes

open_questions.question: Should the refactor land with zero visible behavior change, or bundle the obvious v0.1 paper cuts (execCommand, global event)?

options.label: Bundle the paper cuts (Recommended)
options.info: Both are 5-line fixes inside layers being rewritten anyway; separate PRs cost more than the risk.

options.label: Strict zero-change refactor
options.info: Purest diff but forces touching Platform twice.

tasks.description: Define workspace state object (schemaVersion 2) and Store namespace with actions: setImage, addPoint, undoPoint, clearCurrent, closePolygon, deletePolygon, clearPolygons, addCamera, deleteCamera, plus subscribe/notify
Extract View namespace: render(workspace) for canvas (deriving px from normalized coords against current image dims), renderItemList, renderCamList; wire as Store subscriber
Extract Yaml.generate(workspace) and Platform adapter (readImageFile, copyText with clipboard API + fallback, downloadFile); rewire all buttons through Store/Platform
Write migrateV1() mapping v0.1 in-memory shapes (mode/object_mask, px-bearing points) to schemaVersion 2; fix switchTab global-event usage
Run v0.1 parity checklist (AC1 workflows) and air-gap check (AC5) against refactored file

docs_md:
## State Refactor
Reorganizes the single-file app into Store/View/Yaml/Validate(empty shell)/Persist(empty shell)/Platform namespaces. Normalized coordinates become canonical; pixels are derived at render. All later v0.2 specs assume this structure. No user-visible feature changes except clipboard API modernization.

stub_suggestions.purpose: Central state + pub/sub; every later spec adds actions here
The Electron swap boundary — keep it tiny and async

metrics: 100% of AC1 parity checklist items pass (QA-run, zero regressions)
0 direct mutations of workspace state outside Store (verified by grep for 'state.' writes outside the Store section)
0 network requests and 0 console errors when opened from file:// (DevTools network/console audit)

decision_log.rationale: Every v0.2 feature reads or writes shared state; without a single mutation path, point-editing, persistence, import, and multi-camera would each invent their own state handling and conflict. Normalized-canonical coordinates fix the v0.1 frozen-at-click-time class of bugs before three features (drag, import, multi-camera) would each trip over it.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: skip refactor, build features on v0.1 globals — each feature's diff grows and multi-camera becomes a rewrite anyway
Rejected: ES modules + multiple files — violates single-file constraint
Rejected: introduce a microframework (Preact/petite-vue) — violates no-framework constraint, overkill for this UI
tradeoffs: Refactor delivers zero user-visible value on its own; one sprint slot spent on foundation
Plain-object namespaces rely on convention, not enforcement
decision_log.confidence_rationale: v0.1 is 808 lines and fully understood; the target structure is dictated by the locked architecture; parity is mechanically checkable.

defaults_applied: Assuming vanilla JavaScript, not TypeScript, per master spec v0.1–v0.2 stack
Assuming 'default' pseudo-camera holds polygons until multi-camera (spec 08) introduces real camera binding
Assuming crypto.randomUUID() is available (all evergreen browsers; file:// counts as secure context for it)
